id: Angels including Uriel, Raphael, Michael, Gabriel
name: Angels including Uriel, Raphael, Michael, Gabriel
shortDescription: Angels in Jewish tradition are celestial beings who serve as messengers and agents of God, embodying divine attributes and carrying out God's will.
description: In Jewish mythology and theology, angels are celestial beings created by God to serve various functions, most notably as messengers, warriors, and guardians. The names Uriel, Raphael, Michael, and Gabriel represent some of the most prominent angels within this hierarchy. Michael, whose name means "Who is like God?", is often depicted as the chief of the angels, a powerful warrior, and protector of Israel. Gabriel, meaning "God is my strength," is primarily known as the messenger angel, delivering important announcements, such as the prophecy to Daniel and the annunciation to Mary in Christian tradition (drawing from Jewish roots). Raphael, "God has healed," is associated with healing and is featured prominently in the Book of Tobit. Uriel, "God is my light," is often associated with knowledge and wisdom, serving as an interpreter of prophecies and a guide. The role of angels extends beyond simple communication; they are involved in the cosmic order, the administration of justice, and the protection of individuals and communities. The concept of angels evolved over time, influenced by interactions with other cultures and religious traditions. While the Hebrew Bible mentions angels, their roles and characteristics are further elaborated in post-biblical literature, including the Apocrypha, Pseudepigrapha (such as the Book of Enoch), and Kabbalah. The belief in angels reflects a complex understanding of the divine realm and its interaction with the human world, reinforcing the idea of God's active presence and intervention in human affairs. Their significance lies not only in their individual roles but also in their collective representation of divine attributes and the ongoing relationship between God and creation. The hierarchy and specific attributes of angels vary across different Jewish texts and traditions.

sources.title: Dictionary of Jewish Lore and Legend
sources.author: Alan Unterman
